acceptance_criteria: Events broadcast to all subscribers of matching channel; different channel types are isolated; slow receiver gets Lagged error not panic; 100 concurrent subscribers work
description:
Implement StreamingHub that manages broadcast channels for all streaming channel types. Channel types: Public, PublicLocal, User(account_id), Hashtag(String), Direct(account_id). Each channel is a tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<StreamEvent>. StreamingHub is shared via Arc in axum State. Clients subscribe by calling hub.subscribe(ChannelKey) → Receiver<StreamEvent>.

AGENT WORKFLOW: Spawn a research subagent to analyze tokio broadcast channel semantics (what happens to slow receivers, what's the right channel capacity, how to handle lagged receivers). Spawn a testing subagent to write concurrency tests.

TEST DISCIPLINE:
- Subscribe to Public channel → receive events published to Public channel
- Subscribe to User(123) channel → do NOT receive events published to User(456) channel
- Slow receiver: test that a receiver lagging behind by more than capacity receives a RecvError::Lagged — and the SSE handler degrades gracefully (sends reconnect hint, not panic)
- 100 concurrent subscribers to same channel: all receive the same event

DEFENSIVE PROGRAMMING:
- Channel capacity: 256 events (configurable) — large enough to buffer a brief slow subscriber, small enough to limit memory
- Use DashMap for concurrent access to channel map — not RwLock<HashMap> (avoids write lock on every subscribe)
- Channel cleanup: remove channels with no active senders (but keep channels alive while server runs — they're lightweight)
title: StreamingHub: tokio broadcast channels keyed by channel type
